findings.evidence: The specialized inverse lemma is only lines 87-90, while the existing general API exposes the scalar-aware form `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_tmul_single` at `TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/DiagonalizableGroupBaseChange.lean:102`, described as evaluating `s ⊗ single g r`.
findings.fix: Add a public `@[simp]` lemma for `((baseChangePointsMulEquiv ... n).symm ζ).ofConv (s ⊗ₜ[k] MonoidAlgebra.single (generator n) r) = s • (r • ((ζ : Aˣ) : A))`, and derive the current normalized lemma from it if desired.
findings.issue: `baseChangePointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_single_generator` only characterizes the inverse point on `1 ⊗ single (generator n) 1`, so users cannot rewrite the same base-changed generator with arbitrary `K`/`k` coefficients without unfolding the equivalence or dropping to the general diagonalizable API.

summary: The new equivalence has the basic generator readback API, but the inverse API stops at the normalized tensor `1 ⊗ single generator 1` and leaves the base-change scalar behavior uncharacterized.
